payload.query: Important work in comparative politics has found that political institutions help explain variation in various outcomes across cases, including regime stability, accountability, participation, partisan majorities in parliament, and economic growth. Considering the breadth of research in this area, discuss the institutions that have been proven to be more important.

articles.title: Why the Epstein Investigations Took So Long and Did So Little
articles.abstract: Decades of tips and investigations yielded charges against only two people. A combination of missed chances, narrow laws and prosecutors’ limited focus helps explain why.
articles.snippet: Decades of tips and investigations yielded charges against only two people. A combination of missed chances, narrow laws and prosecutors’ limited focus helps explain why.
articles.byline: By Benjamin Weiser, Matthew Goldstein and Mike Baker

articles.title: Divide Among Supreme Court’s Conservatives Could Test Trump’s Agenda
articles.abstract: In rejecting President Trump’s tariffs, the court’s six conservative justices displayed subtle differences in their views of executive power.
articles.snippet: In rejecting President Trump’s tariffs, the court’s six conservative justices displayed subtle differences in their views of executive power.

articles.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War
articles.abstract: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.
articles.snippet: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.
articles.byline: By Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Edward Wong, Eric Schmitt and Ronen Bergman
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

articles.title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
articles.abstract: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.snippet: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.byline: By Jim Tankersley, Aaron Boxerman, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

articles.title: Inside the Underground Safe Houses Sheltering Immigrants From ICE
articles.abstract: In Springfield, Ohio, some Americans have converted their basements and spare bedrooms into shelters for immigrant families who could be targeted in raids.
articles.snippet: In Springfield, Ohio, some Americans have converted their basements and spare bedrooms into shelters for immigrant families who could be targeted in raids.
articles.byline: By Miriam Jordan and Maddie McGarvey

articles.title: Trump, the Self-Declared Peace President, Goes to War Seeking Regime Change
articles.abstract: President Trump has become increasingly willing to assert American power overseas, a decade after propelling himself to the highest office by promising to focus on “America first.”
articles.snippet: President Trump has become increasingly willing to assert American power overseas, a decade after propelling himself to the highest office by promising to focus on “America first.”

articles.title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
articles.abstract: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
articles.snippet: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.

articles.title: How Kennedy Is Trying to Revamp Medical School
articles.abstract: The health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has tapped into an old debate about how much doctors should know about nutrition. But some of his ideas, and tactics, concern medical experts.
articles.snippet: The health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has tapped into an old debate about how much doctors should know about nutrition. But some of his ideas, and tactics, concern medical experts.
articles.byline: By Alan Blinder, Alice Callahan and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

articles.title: Welcome to Wyoming, the Frontier of America’s New Gilded Age
articles.abstract: Jackson, Wyo., has long been a refuge for the rich. But the last five years saw a boom in wealth of a kind never before seen. Across the country, the 2017 tax cuts minted hundreds of new billionaires.
articles.snippet: Jackson, Wyo., has long been a refuge for the rich. But the last five years saw a boom in wealth of a kind never before seen. Across the country, the 2017 tax cuts minted hundreds of new billionaires.
articles.byline: By Katie Benner, Steven Rich, Mike Baker, John Branch and Will Warasila

articles.title: Congestion Pricing Wins in Court After Lengthy Battle With Trump
articles.abstract: A judge ruled to preserve the toll, ending, for now, a dispute that has become emblematic of New York’s resistance to federal intervention in local affairs.
articles.snippet: A judge ruled to preserve the toll, ending, for now, a dispute that has become emblematic of New York’s resistance to federal intervention in local affairs.
articles.byline: By Stefanos Chen and Winnie Hu
